bugs.title: All streaming RPCs broken: telemetry.statusWriter does not implement http.Flusher
bugs.description: SendMessage (the only streaming RPC) returns an internal error: '*telemetry.statusWriter does not implement http.Flusher'. This blocks ALL chat functionality -- companion mode, planning mode, and selection mode are completely non-functional. The telemetry.Middleware in internal/telemetry/middleware.go wraps http.ResponseWriter in a statusWriter that has a Flush() method, but ConnectRPC's direct type assertion `responseWriter.(http.Flusher)` fails. This may be caused by the otelhttp.NewHandler wrapping the ResponseWriter in a way that breaks interface satisfaction downstream.
bugs.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Start backend with 'go run ./cmd/server'
2. Create a trip and set to ACTIVE
3. Send any SendMessage RPC via buf curl or curl
4. Observe internal error response
bugs.expected: Streaming response with AI-generated content, tool calls, and stream events
bugs.api_command: buf curl --schema . --protocol connect --http2-prior-knowledge --header 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN' --header 'Origin: http://localhost:3000' -d '{"content":"What is interesting near me?","mode":"CHAT_MODE_COMPANION","trip_id":"...","user_location":{"latitude":41.4036,"longitude":2.1744}}' http://localhost:8090/toqui.v1.ChatService/SendMessage

bugs.title: testctl generates tokens missing required iss/aud claims
bugs.description: The testctl create-user command generates JWT tokens without the 'iss' (toqui) and 'aud' (toqui-api) claims that the auth interceptor now requires. This means all tokens generated by testctl are immediately rejected by the backend with 'token is missing required claim: aud claim is required, token is missing required claim: iss claim is required'. The auth.GenerateAccessToken() method (internal/auth/auth.go:131-141) correctly includes these claims, but testctl (cmd/testctl/main.go:114-119) does not.
bugs.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Run 'go run ./cmd/testctl create-user --name Test --email test@toqui-test.local'
2. Use the returned token with any authenticated RPC
3. Observe unauthenticated error
bugs.expected: Token should include 'iss: toqui' and 'aud: toqui-api' claims
bugs.actual: Token only has sub, exp, iat claims; auth interceptor rejects it

bugs.title: ResolvePersona RPC ignores coordinates and trip destination country
bugs.description: The ResolvePersona handler (internal/handlers/persona.go:80-103) hardcodes regionCode to empty string and never reads the trip's destination_country from the database or reverse-geocodes the provided latitude/longitude. Since the underlying Registry.Resolve() returns the default Toqui persona when regionCode is empty, ResolvePersona always returns Toqui regardless of input. The lat/lng fields in ResolvePersonaRequest are accepted by the proto but completely unused by the handler.
bugs.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Create a trip with destination ES
2. Call ResolvePersona with trip_id, latitude 41.4036, longitude 2.1744, themes ['architecture']
3. Observe that Toqui (global) is returned instead of a Spain+architecture expert
bugs.expected: A Spain + architecture composed expert persona (e.g., Elena or similar)
bugs.api_command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/toqui.v1.PersonaService/ResolvePersona -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Origin: http://localhost:3000' -H 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN' -d '{"trip_id":"...","latitude":41.4036,"longitude":2.1744,"mode":"companion","themes":["architecture"]}'

bugs.title: GetNearby returns generic internal error instead of clear 'no API key' message
bugs.description: When GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY is not configured, the GetNearby RPC returns {"code":"internal","message":"an internal error occurred"} instead of a user-friendly error explaining that the Places API is not available. The server logs show the real error: 'nearby places not available: GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY is not configured' but this is not surfaced to the client. The error should be mapped to a more specific gRPC status code (e.g., Unavailable or FailedPrecondition) with a clear message.
bugs.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Start backend without GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY
2. Call GetNearby with valid coordinates and category
3. Observe generic internal error
bugs.expected: A specific error like {"code":"unavailable","message":"nearby places not available: Google Places API key is not configured"}

ux_issues.description: UpdateLocation returns empty response ({}) with no feedback to the user about whether their location was cached or any error occurred. While this is technically valid (the suggestions field is optional), it would be more useful to return a confirmation or at least the cached location coordinates.
ux_issues.suggestion: Return at least a success acknowledgment or the cached location data in the response

ux_issues.description: The themes field on the trip is auto-detected as ['food', 'history'] based on the description, but the trip description mentioned 'architecture' prominently. The theme detection missed the primary user intent.
ux_issues.suggestion: Improve theme detection to better match explicit mentions in the description (e.g., 'architecture' should be detected when 'Gaudi architecture' is mentioned)

tool_failures.input: CHAT_MODE_COMPANION with user_location, trip_id, content 'What is interesting near me?'
tool_failures.error: *telemetry.statusWriter does not implement http.Flusher -- all streaming blocked
Internal error (server log: GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY is not configured)

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The core chat/companion functionality is completely broken due to a streaming RPC bug where the telemetry middleware's ResponseWriter wrapper fails the http.Flusher interface assertion in ConnectRPC. This P0 bug blocks all AI interaction -- no messages can be sent in any mode. Trip CRUD operations (create, update status, list, share/unshare) work correctly. The testctl tool also generates invalid tokens (missing iss/aud claims), which would block all agentic testing until fixed. The ResolvePersona RPC never resolves to a location-specific expert because it ignores both the coordinates and the trip's destination country. Without chat functionality, the app is unusable as a travel companion.
